message:
fix(database): hoist pool guards, MySQL IPv6+DoS hardening, cancel-aware validation

Pool (pool.rs):
- Hoisted AsyncValidationGuard and AsyncCreationGuard from inside the
  get_async method to module-level structs. This makes them available to
  other pool methods and eliminates re-definition on every call.
- Missing discard counter: return_connection now increments total_discards
  when disconnecting a stale connection. drain_idle similarly counts.
- Cancel-awareness: get_async now checks cx.is_cancel_requested() after
  async validation completes, preventing a validated connection from
  being returned to a cancelled caller.
- Removed unused IdleConnection::new constructor.

MySQL (mysql.rs):
- IPv6 host parsing: unbracketed IPv6 addresses (multiple colons without
  brackets) are now handled without attempting port split, defaulting to
  port 3306 like the Postgres driver already does.
- DoS mitigation: read_packet now enforces a 64MB MAX_PAYLOAD_SIZE limit
  on reassembled multi-packet payloads, preventing unbounded memory growth
  from malicious or buggy packet length headers.
- Cancellation propagation: query and execute now map
  MySqlError::Cancelled to Outcome::Cancelled instead of Outcome::Err,
  allowing the caller to distinguish between real errors and cancellation.

Postgres (postgres.rs):
- Two #[allow(dead_code)] annotations for fields retained for future use.

SQLite (sqlite.rs):
- Removed trailing blank lines.

Transaction (transaction.rs):
- Minor formatting adjustment.

Tests updated to match new pool behavior and error variants.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
